title: duplicate cwd_lock helper inlined verbatim in worktree.rs and cron.rs tests
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
DRY

## Location
src/tools/worktree.rs:349-354 and src/tools/cron.rs:268-273 (both define `fn cwd_lock() -> MutexGuard<'static, ()>`)

## Violation
The exact same helper definition appears in both test modules:

```rust
fn cwd_lock() -> MutexGuard<'static, ()> {
    static LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();
    LOCK.get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
        .lock()
        .unwrap_or_else(std::sync::PoisonError::into_inner)
}
```

Two separate `static LOCK` instances — meaning the cwd_lock in worktree.rs does NOT serialize against the cwd_lock in cron.rs. A worktree test and a cron test that BOTH mutate cwd via std::env::set_current_dir can interleave under parallel test execution and corrupt each other's cwd assumptions.

(The comment in cron.rs:267 even acknowledges the need to coordinate "with worktree tests" — but the two locks are independent.)

## Mandated refactor
Move the helper to a shared test utility module (e.g. `src/tools/testutil.rs` with `#[cfg(test)] pub`) that exports a single `pub fn process_cwd_lock() -> MutexGuard<'static, ()>` backed by ONE OnceLock<Mutex<()>>. Both test modules import that helper. This guarantees mutual exclusion across the entire process.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
